sciCentRverse 0.2.0
    • Camera Trap Density Workflow
  • Reference

Consolidate per-image species tags into one row from a WildTrax main report

Source: R/cam_consolidate_tags.R
cam_consolidate_tags.Rd

For each (project_id, location, location_id, image_id, species_common_name) group, sum individual_count (numeric rows) and collapse age_class / sex_class by replicating each tag by that row's count and joining with ", ". The image_date_time is retained and must be identical within each image. Non-species rows (e.g., "STAFF/SETUP", "NONE") are preserved unchanged. Rows whose species_common_name is neither in native_sp nor in the non-species list are dropped with a warning listing the unexpected labels and row counts.

Usage

cam_consolidate_tags(report)

Arguments

report

A data.frame/tibble with at least: project_id, location, location_id, image_id, image_date_time, species_common_name, individual_count, age_class, sex_class, tag_id. Should be a main report from WildTrax.

Value

A tibble with one row per image × species: project_id, location, location_id, image_id, image_date_time, species_common_name, age_class, sex_class, individual_count (character).

Details

Species identification uses the internal character vector native_sp saved in R/sysdata.rda.

The function also detects, warns about, and removes likely duplicate tag entries where all fields are identical except tag_id, which is a possible double-entry flag. The duplicate check is applied to species rows only, using these fields: project_id, location, location_id, image_id, image_date_time, species_common_name, individual_count, age_class, sex_class. When duplicates are found, the function keeps one row per identical set (the one with the lowest tag_id) and drops the rest.

See also

cam_calc_time_by_series() for the next step in the pipeline.

Author

Marcus Becker

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
x <- cam_consolidate_tags(main_reports)
# If duplicate tags were removed, you'll see a warning listing image_id(s).
} # }
